what_it_does: This file is a metadata record that links a channel attachment (att-6a4eb455) in the dragnet-development channel to a specific board item (board://item-a402a3a1).
observations: YAML frontmatter only, with no body prose
Target is a board:// URI pointing to item-a402a3a1
standouts: Target uses a board:// URI scheme rather than a file path or URL
The entire content is frontmatter with no explanatory body
It records a precise attachment creation timestamp
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields
Stores channel attachment metadata as a standalone markdown file
References an external board item via a custom URI scheme
concerns: No explanation of what is being attached or why
The semantics of attachment_type board_items are not defined in this file
The referenced board item is not described here, so the linkage lacks local context
notes: A bare attachment linkage record that connects a dragnet-development channel attachment to a board item by URI.
questions: What content or artifact does this attachment represent?
What does board item item-a402a3a1 contain or discuss?
What is the intended meaning of attachment_type board_items?
Who or what created this attachment?
purpose_vs_actual: No explicit purpose is stated; the file functions as a timestamped linkage record between an attachment and a board item.
intention_signals: To permanently record that attachment att-6a4eb455 in the dragnet-development channel is associated with board item item-a402a3a1.
novelty: The use of a board:// URI scheme to target a board item is the most distinctive feature; otherwise it is a conventional metadata record.
contribution: Provides a persistent, timestamped attachment-to-board-item reference that a fused system could use to reconstruct channel-board relationships.
summary_for_embedding: Markdown metadata record linking channel attachment att-6a4eb455 in dragnet-development to board item board://item-a402a3a1, with an added timestamp and no body content.
intention_for_embedding: To record that a specific channel attachment is attached to a particular board item.
